normalization: line-ending-normalized (CR bytes stripped) before hashing, so the pin is reproducible across Windows (CRLF) and Linux (LF) checkouts
note: Tamper-evidence integrity pins for the governance constitution the IX pipeline gate enforces (RSI safe-loop research 2026-06-07; reference_safe_rsi_loop_validated). Each value is `sha256:<hex>` over the LINE-ENDING-NORMALIZED content (CR stripped) of the constitution at governance/demerzel/constitutions/<name> (the file lives in the Demerzel SUBMODULE; this pin lives in IX's own tree so IX asserts which constitution it was reviewed against). The gate (crates/ix-skill governance_gate + ConstitutionGate::load) fails CLOSED on mismatch; a missing entry is reported as `unpinned` (never silently trusted); a present-but-corrupt pin file also fails closed. Regenerate ONLY for a deliberate, reviewed constitution change, normalizing line endings: `tr -d '\r' < <file> | sha256sum` (or `git show HEAD:constitutions/<name> | tr -d '\r' | sha256sum` for the committed blob). Do NOT use a raw `Get-FileHash` — it would hash CRLF on Windows and never match the LF blob CI sees. An undeclared edit (no re-pin) is exactly what this catches — both at runtime and in CI via default_constitution_matches_committed_pin.
